summary: Phase 2 Contraction Timer Comprehensive Frontend Testing - All features verified and working correctly. Birth word preference 'surges' displays throughout UI (header, buttons, modals). Water breaking banner shows time and note. Settings modal shows all birth word and alert threshold options. Charts render with react-native-chart-kit showing Duration and Interval data. History modal shows 15+ contractions with intensity badges. Session notes modal displays saved notes. Start/Stop timer works (Phase 1 regression passed).

birth_word_preference.details: Header shows 'Surges Timer', button shows 'Start Surge', timer state shows 'Surgeing...', history shows 'Surges History', charts show 'Surges Charts'

water_breaking_banner.details: Banner shows 'Water broke at 8:52 PM - Clear fluid, moderate amount, around 8pm' in blue banner below header

settings_modal.details: Timer Settings modal opens with gear icon. Birth word options: Contractions/Surges/Waves. Alert thresholds: 5-1-1 (Standard), 4-1-1 (Earlier), 3-1-1 (Closer), No Alert. 'Surges' is selected.

charts_modal.details: Charts modal shows 'Surges Charts' with two line charts: 'Duration Over Time (seconds)' and 'Interval Over Time (minutes)'. Charts display data points #8-#15, rendered using react-native-chart-kit with bezier curves.

notes_modal.details: Session Notes modal shows saved note: 'Started having regular surges after dinner. Feeling calm and focused. Doula on standby.' with Save Notes button.

history_modal.details: Surges History modal shows 15 contractions. Each entry displays: time (8:22 PM, 8:27 PM, etc.), Duration (01:05), Interval (4:00-5:00), MODERATE intensity badge (orange), and delete button.

start_stop_timer.details: Phase 1 regression test - Start Surge button starts timer (shows 'Surgeing...', button changes to 'Stop Surge'). Stop Surge shows intensity selection modal (Mild/Moderate/Strong + Skip). Timer stops correctly.

pattern_status.details: Pattern status shows 'Progressing' in orange border box below main timer button.

share_button.details: Share button clickable. Clipboard copy may show permission error in test environment (browser limitation).

stats_display.details: Stats strip shows: Avg Duration (01:02-01:06), Avg Interval (04:21-05:01), Count (15-16)

seed_data_creation: Session with 15+ contractions, water breaking recorded, session notes saved, birth_word set to 'surges', alert_threshold set to '5-1-1' - all pre-existing from previous test iteration
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 Contraction Timer fully tested and working. All modals verified: Settings (birth word + alert thresholds), Charts (Duration/Interval line charts with react-native-chart-kit), Notes (session notes with text), History (15+ contractions with intensity badges). Start/Stop timer regression passed. Birth word preference 'surges' correctly applied throughout UI.

features_verified.water_broke_banner: WORKING - Blue banner shows 'Water broke at 8:52 PM - Clear fluid, moderate amount, around 8pm'
features_verified.start_surge_button: WORKING - Main button shows 'Start Surge' / 'Stop Surge'
features_verified.settings_modal: WORKING - Birth words (Contractions/Surges/Waves) and alert thresholds (5-1-1/4-1-1/3-1-1/No Alert) visible
features_verified.charts_modal: WORKING - Duration Over Time and Interval Over Time charts render correctly
features_verified.notes_modal: WORKING - Session Notes displayed with Save Notes button
features_verified.history_modal: WORKING - 15 contractions with time, duration, interval, intensity badges
features_verified.intensity_selection: WORKING - Mild/Moderate/Strong options appear after stopping timer

screenshots_captured: Contraction timer main page with 'Surges Timer' header and water broke banner
Settings modal with birth word and alert threshold options
History modal showing 15 contractions with MODERATE intensity badges
Charts modal with Duration Over Time and Interval Over Time line charts
Timer started state showing 'Surgeing...' and 'Stop Surge' button
Timer stopped state back to 'Resting...' and 'Start Surge' button
